description: External Event Detection - Create kernel/event_detector.py that detects external file changes (user-modified rules, prompts, notes) by comparing file mtimes against state.yaml last_updated. Add user_owned_files tracking to prevent evolution from overwriting user edits.
steps: Create kernel/event_detector.py with class EventDetector(kernel_root: Path): method detect_external_changes(last_updated: str) -> list[dict] that: scans key directories (kernel/prompts/, knowledge/rules/, memory/) for files modified after last_updated timestamp, returns list of event dicts with keys: type ('prompt_modified', 'new_rule_added', 'note_left', 'manual_task_added'), path, modified_at
Add method get_user_owned_files(state: dict) -> list[str] that reads state['user_owned_files'] list (or empty list if not present)
Add method mark_user_owned(state: dict, file_path: str) -> None that appends file_path to state['user_owned_files'] list

Add detect logic: compare file mtime (as ISO string) against last_updated from state.yaml. Categorize by directory: kernel/prompts/ -> 'prompt_modified', knowledge/rules/ -> 'new_rule_added', memory/*.md -> 'note_left', memory/tasks.yaml -> 'manual_task_added' (if mtime > last_updated)
Integrate with EvolutionEngine: in validate_change(), if the change targets a file in user_owned_files, reject it with reason 'File is user-owned and cannot be modified by evolution'

Add tests in tests/test_event_detector.py: test detection of modified prompt files, test detection of new rules, test user_owned_files prevents evolution changes, test no false positives when nothing changed, test mark_user_owned adds to list
acceptance_criteria: detect_external_changes() correctly identifies files modified after last_updated
Events are properly categorized by type based on directory
user_owned_files integration prevents evolution from overwriting user edits
findings: All 876 tests pass with 98.87% coverage. validate_change signature updated with backward-compatible state parameter. 26 new tests added covering all acceptance criteria.
